evidence_generated: Repeated-seed AUROC/AUPRC/F1/ECE/Brier tables, AUROC CIs, weekly AUROC figures, and threshold-optimized failure F1 using out-of-fold training predictions.
remaining_caveat_for_manuscript: Do not claim universal predictive superiority. Safe claim: the stable-causal-temporal model is competitive while enabling later causal-effect and actionability analyses.

evidence_generated: Doubly robust temporally ordered ATE estimates with bootstrap sign stability and a stable behavior-outcome graph.
remaining_caveat_for_manuscript: Causal interpretation is conditional on temporal precedence, observed-confounder exchangeability, overlap, and correct nuisance modelling. Hidden confounding remains possible.

evidence_generated: Cross-course transfer table, AUROC/ECE/Brier trade-off summary with bootstrap CI, and synthetic shift sensitivity.
remaining_caveat_for_manuscript: Cross-course AUROC transfer is mixed and sometimes negative. Frame RQ3 as an accuracy-calibration robustness trade-off rather than universal transfer improvement.

evidence_generated: Model-recomputed feasible recourse with validity, plausibility, sparsity, effort, and risk reduction.
remaining_caveat_for_manuscript: Counterfactuals are algorithmic model-space recourse, not guaranteed real-world intervention effects. Some high-risk cases may not have sparse valid recourse under the chosen constraints.

evidence_generated: Weekly readiness metrics, threshold utility surface, and utility sensitivity analysis across multiple TP/FP/FN assumptions.
remaining_caveat_for_manuscript: Utility weights are institutional decision assumptions. Report sensitivity results and avoid claiming one universally optimal intervention week unless stable across scenarios.

evidence_generated: Separate versus joint metrics, bootstrap AUROC difference, calibration curves, risk-space segmentation, and feature contribution heatmap.
remaining_caveat_for_manuscript: If joint AUROC is similar or lower, frame the contribution as intervention taxonomy, calibration, and multi-outcome segmentation rather than predictive superiority.

evidence_generated: Capacity-aware triage using true labels, student-level expected intervention benefit, bootstrap policy differences, fixed-recall precision, and real subgroup audit with Wilson CIs.
remaining_caveat_for_manuscript: Causal-actionable ranking should be interpreted as intervention-oriented prioritization. It may trade some pure precision at low capacity for greater expected intervention benefit.
